metadata.source: 2026 Budget Review (25 February 2026), National Treasury South Africa; 2025/26 Estimates of National Expenditure (May 2025 Budget); Parliamentary Committee BRRRs
metadata.notes: Figures updated to 2025/26 from the 2026 Budget Review (tabled 25 Feb 2026) and May 2025 ENE. 'Allocated' figures reflect 2025/26 budget appropriations or multi-year programme envelopes current as of the 2026 Budget Review. 'Recommended' figures represent Parliamentary committee BRRR recommendations and independent estimates. Key macro context: consolidated expenditure R2.58 trillion in 2025/26; deficit 4.5% of GDP; debt-to-GDP 78.9% (projected peak, first debt stabilisation in 17 years); primary surplus 0.9% of GDP.

packages.summary: Infrastructure spending is the dominant priority of the 2025/26 budget, with R526.3 billion committed over the MTEF for water and roads alone. The Eskom conditional equity conversion (R254bn, 2023–2026) is entering its final tranche. Grid expansion, PRASA recapitalisation, and bulk water infrastructure have all seen real increases, but fall short of committee-recommended levels for full backlogs.

key_line_items.item: Eskom Debt Relief (conditional equity conversion — final tranche)
key_line_items.department: Department of Public Enterprises / National Treasury
key_line_items.notes: R254bn conditional equity conversion announced in 2023 Budget, structured across 2023/24–2025/26. Programme is in its final tranche. Load-shedding largely resolved. Source: National Treasury Budget Review 2026.

key_line_items.item: PRASA Capital Injection and Fleet Recapitalisation
key_line_items.notes: Increased from R9.9bn to R11.5bn in 2025/26 ENE. Sanral maintaining 27,000km and resurfacing 2,000km of road network. Portfolio Committee recommended R18.5bn to address full fleet backlog and signal upgrades. Source: 2026 Budget Review; May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Renewable Energy Transmission Grid Expansion
key_line_items.department: Department of Mineral Resources and Energy / Eskom Transmission
key_line_items.notes: Updated to R19.2bn for 2025/26, reflecting accelerated grid investment as renewable energy pipeline grows post-load-shedding resolution. Energy Portfolio Committee recommended R42bn over 5 years. Source: 2026 Budget Review; 2025/26 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Water Infrastructure Investment (bulk supply and wastewater)
key_line_items.notes: Significantly increased from R8.2bn to R21.0bn in 2025/26, reflecting the 2026 Budget's emphasis on water and roads as the dominant infrastructure priority (R526.3bn over MTEF for water and roads combined). Recommended figure raised to R35bn per DWS infrastructure backlog estimates. Source: 2026 Budget Review; Parliament of SA education/health/infrastructure budget brief.

key_line_items.item: Transnet Operational Turnaround Support
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R14.0bn in 2025/26. Includes equity support and operational grants. DPE committee BRRR recommended R22bn for locomotive fleet and maintenance. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Digital Infrastructure — SA Connect Phase 2
key_line_items.department: Department of Communications and Digital Technologies
key_line_items.notes: R2.6bn allocated vs R8bn recommended to connect all public institutions and reach 80% household broadband. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Municipal Infrastructure Grant (water and sanitation components)
key_line_items.notes: MIG allocation increased to R17.0bn in 2025/26 in line with the infrastructure push. Committee recommended uplift to address backlogs in O&M alongside capital. Source: 2026 Budget Review; May 2025 ENE.

packages.summary: SMME and employment programmes remain the most severely underfunded of the five packages relative to committee recommendations. Youth employment and informal economy support fall far short of the scale needed to address structural unemployment (~32% broad rate). The SRD grant was extended by R35.2bn to March 2026, but labour-market activation programmes lag. Source: 2026 Budget Review.

key_line_items.item: SEFA (Small Enterprise Finance Agency) Capitalisation
key_line_items.notes: Portfolio Committee on Small Business Development repeatedly recommended higher capitalisation to expand SMME lending book. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: SMME Red Tape Reduction / BizPortal Integration
key_line_items.notes: Primarily an IT and operational reform — low fiscal cost but underfunded relative to system integration targets.

key_line_items.item: Youth Employment Intervention — YES Programme Matching
key_line_items.notes: Government matching for YES (Youth Employment Service). Modest increase in 2025/26. Committee recommended scaling to 1 million placements. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Expanded Public Works Programme (EPWP)
key_line_items.department: Department of Public Works and Infrastructure
key_line_items.notes: R6.6bn allocated in 2025/26; committee recommended expansion and transition to skills-linked programmes. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Informal Economy Trading Infrastructure and Support
key_line_items.notes: Trading space upgrades, registration support and working capital for informal traders. Severely underfunded.

key_line_items.item: SMME Procurement Reform — Set-Asides Monitoring
key_line_items.notes: Compliance monitoring and supplier database. Primarily a governance reform.

key_line_items.item: Township and Rural Entrepreneurship Programme (TREP)
key_line_items.notes: Spatial-targeted enterprise development. Committee recommended significant scale-up. Source: May 2025 ENE.

packages.summary: Education and health see real increases in 2025/26 — education remains the largest function at 23.7% of consolidated spending over the MTEF. ECD receives an additional R12.8bn over 3 years (R4.3bn/year). Provincial health receives R298.9bn in 2025/26 including R26bn for HIV/AIDS programmes over the MTEF. NSFAS and NHI preparation both see increased allocations, though NHI pilot infrastructure remains below Phase 1 requirements. Source: 2026 Budget Review.

key_line_items.item: National Reading and Literacy Crisis Response
key_line_items.notes: Basic Education committee BRRR recommended a dedicated reading crisis programme. 2025/26 allocation modestly increased. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: TVET College Recapitalisation and Curriculum Modernisation
key_line_items.department: Department of Higher Education and Training
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R14.1bn in 2025/26. DHET committee consistently recommends uplift for infrastructure and lecturer quality upgrades. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Early Childhood Development (ECD) Phase 1 — Subsidy Expansion
key_line_items.department: Department of Basic Education (post transfer from Social Development)
key_line_items.notes: Updated to R4.3bn for 2025/26 (R12.8bn over the 3-year MTEF, expanding services to an additional 300,000 children). Source: 2026 Budget Review — ECD grant addition confirmed.

key_line_items.item: NHI Implementation — Phase 1 Pilot Infrastructure
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R4.0bn in 2025/26 as NHI preparations advance following the NHI Act's promulgation. Health committee noted this remains insufficient for Phase 1 primary care contracting. R22bn estimate from NHI fiscal frameworks tabled in committee. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS)
key_line_items.department: Department of Higher Education and Training
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R19.1bn in 2025/26. NSFAS reform and expanded eligibility drive higher recommended figure. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Advanced Manufacturing / Green Transition Skills Programme
key_line_items.department: Department of Higher Education and Training / DTIC
key_line_items.notes: EV, solar, green hydrogen artisan pipeline. Committee noted R4bn needed for 50 000 transition artisans over 5 years. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Primary Health Care Platform Strengthening (PHC revitalisation)
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R8.5bn in 2025/26. Provincial health total reaches R298.9bn (2025/26), with R21.3bn allocated over MTEF for compensation of doctors and goods/services shortfalls. Health committee BRRR recommended R12bn for full clinic revitalisation and community health worker formalisation. Source: 2026 Budget Review.

key_line_items.item: TB and Communicable Disease Control
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R3.0bn in 2025/26. R26bn allocated over MTEF for HIV/AIDS (prevention of mother-to-child transmission and ARVs). Portfolio Committee noted underfunding relative to 2025 TB Elimination targets. Source: 2026 Budget Review.

packages.summary: Industrial policy incentives and trade facilitation remain significantly underfunded. DTIC incentive programmes have faced successive budget pressure even as AfCFTA implementation requires scale-up and the EV transition creates urgency around the APDP. Critical minerals beneficiation still lacks a dedicated investment vehicle. Source: 2025/26 ENE; 2026 Budget Review.

key_line_items.item: DTIC Industrial Incentives (MCEP, EMIA, Black Industrialists)
key_line_items.department: Department of Trade, Industry and Competition
key_line_items.notes: Increased modestly to R5.5bn in 2025/26. Trade & Industry committee BRRR continues to note cuts relative to peak incentive years; R9.8bn needed to restore pipeline. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Critical Minerals Beneficiation — Catalytic Investment Fund
key_line_items.department: Department of Mineral Resources and Energy
key_line_items.notes: Slight increase to R0.6bn. No dedicated beneficiation fund exists; IDC support continues. Committee recommended R4.5bn catalytic vehicle. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: AfCFTA Implementation — Trade Facilitation and Capacity
key_line_items.department: Department of Trade, Industry and Competition
key_line_items.notes: AfCFTA secretariat and NTB resolution mechanisms. Severely underfunded relative to opportunity. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Automotive Production and Development Programme (APDP Phase 2)
key_line_items.department: Department of Trade, Industry and Competition
key_line_items.notes: Automotive master plan support. Committee recommended R5bn to cover EV transition incentive top-up. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Special Economic Zones Capital and Infrastructure
key_line_items.department: Department of Trade, Industry and Competition
key_line_items.notes: SEZ capex allocation increased to R1.7bn; committee recommended uplift tied to Coega, dti-campus, and Richards Bay expansions. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Export Market Development and Brand SA
key_line_items.department: Department of Trade, Industry and Competition
key_line_items.notes: EMIA and Brand SA combined. Committee recommended scale-up to support AfCFTA market entry. Source: May 2025 ENE.

packages.summary: Governance reform receives relatively better funding given lower fiscal cost, and the 2025/26 budget continues to prioritise SARS modernisation and anti-corruption capacity. South Africa formally exited FATF greylisting in October 2024. SARS capacity expansion is ongoing with the tax-to-GDP ratio rising to 25.9% in 2025/26 (from 25.1% in 2024/25), boosting revenue by R21.3bn above the 2025 Budget estimate. Source: 2026 Budget Review.

key_line_items.item: SARS Capacity Expansion (staff, systems, audit capability)
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R3.8bn in 2025/26. Tax-to-GDP rises to 25.9% (2025/26) from 25.1% (2024/25), with gross tax revenue revised upward by R21.3bn vs 2025 Budget. Finance committee noted SARS needs R5.5bn to restore full staff complement, upgrade IT, and close the high-value tax gap. Source: 2026 Budget Review.

key_line_items.item: National Prosecuting Authority Capacity (NPA + SCCU)
key_line_items.department: Department of Justice and Constitutional Development
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R2.6bn in 2025/26. NPA and Specialised Commercial Crimes Unit. Justice committee recommended uplift for state capture trial infrastructure. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Special Investigating Unit (SIU) and Procurement Reform
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R1.1bn. SIU recoveries far exceed its budget; committee recommended expansion proportional to referral backlog. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Government Technology Modernisation (GITOC / e-government)
key_line_items.department: Department of Communications and Digital Technologies / DPSA
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R2.9bn. Includes Home Affairs digital ID rollout, shared IT infrastructure. Committee recommended accelerated rollout. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: Public Service Commission and DPSA Professionalisation
key_line_items.department: Department of Public Service and Administration
key_line_items.notes: Competency frameworks, SMS salary reform, and performance management systems. Lower cost but chronically under-resourced. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: AGSA Audit and Accountability Infrastructure
key_line_items.notes: Material irregularity follow-up capacity. Close to recommended but still below target. Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: SOE Oversight and Reform Programme (DPE / DPME coordination)
key_line_items.notes: Increased to R5.8bn. Operational grants and turnaround support for SOE portfolio excluding Eskom (covered under Package 1). Source: May 2025 ENE.

key_line_items.item: FATF Exit Legislative Package Implementation
key_line_items.notes: SA formally exited FATF greylisting in October 2024. Primarily legislative reform with minimal direct fiscal cost — implementation complete. Source: FATF, October 2024.
